description: Currently receiving stable LTL income and fears losing it by switching to STR. The potential gain must be made to feel safe, not just larger. Pain of losing current income outweighs excitement about projected gains.
top_fears: STR income less reliable than current LTL rent
void periods leave me worse off than I am now
guests will damage property more than tenants
the switch is irreversible and I will regret it
my current tenant may leave and I cannot get a new one
top_questions: What is your average occupancy rate?
What happens in January and slow months?
How does your income compare to my current rent?
Can I switch back to LTL if STR does not work?
What are your void periods like?
what_resonates: showing STR net income is materially higher than their current LTL
seasonal income data showing winter floors
framing STR trial as reversible decision
zero upfront cost removes the risk of trying
month by month actual payout data not just projections
positioning current LTL rent as the floor not the ceiling
what_to_avoid: optimistic income projections without seasonal context
claiming STR is always better than LTL
pressure tactics that increase perceived risk
ideal_response_pattern: Validate the current arrangement then show the gap between LTL and STR net clearly then frame the switch as low-risk and reversible then give a seasonal floor so they know the worst case
closing_approach: Show STR net income vs their current LTL side by side with real figures. Propose a start date after tenant exits. Emphasise zero setup cost.
typical_blockers: current tenant has not yet given notice
worried about void periods in winter
partner wants to keep the certainty of LTL
follow_up_strategy: Send a month-by-month income estimate for their area with seasonal lows shown explicitly. Subject: Your numbers, best and worst case. This removes the unknown that scares them.

notable_leads.name: Avik Pal
notable_leads.note: Current tenant. Classic loss aversion. Needs tenant exit before switching.

notable_leads.name: Magdalena
notable_leads.note: Also betrayal-damaged. Double archetype. Lost previous agent trust AND fears income loss.

notable_leads.name: Noemi
notable_leads.note: Part of multi-lead entry 038. Currently LTL. Responded to income gap numbers.

notable_leads.name: Afnan
notable_leads.note: Needs tenant exit. Strong candidate once clear.
